task_completion.description: Load the 'SAMPLE_ORDER_LINES' dataset in Oracle Analytics Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: Oracle Analytics Desktop is open with the 'SAMPLE_ORDER_LINES' dataset visible in the Data Elements panel on the left.

task_completion.description: Select the Waterfall chart visualization type
task_completion.visual_evidence: The visualization type icon or dropdown in the grammar panel indicates 'Waterfall' is selected.
The 'Profit' column is dragged into the Values (or Y-axis/Measure) section of the visualization grammar panel.

task_completion.description: Use 'Product Sub Category' for the chart breakdown
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Product Sub Category' column is dragged into the Category (or X-axis/Dimension) section of the visualization grammar panel.

task_completion.description: Render the waterfall chart showing positive and negative contributions
task_completion.visual_evidence: A waterfall chart is visible on the main canvas, displaying a series of floating columns (typically color-coded for increase/decrease) that represent the profit breakdown by product sub-category.

task_completion.description: Save the workbook as 'Profit_Waterfall.dva' in the Documents folder
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog showing the path 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents' and the filename 'Profit_Waterfall', or a file explorer window showing 'Profit_Waterfall.dva' in the Documents folder after saving.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Oracle Analytics Desktop GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the Oracle Analytics interface (menus, drag-and-drop) rather than using terminal commands to generate files.

integrity.description: The visualization is genuinely rendered by the software
integrity.visual_evidence: The chart appears inside the Oracle Analytics canvas with interactive elements visible (like tooltips or selection handles), not as a pasted image or a mockup in a different program.

integrity.description: The file was saved using the application's native save functionality
integrity.visual_evidence: The Oracle Analytics 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog is used, rather than a script or text editor creating a dummy .dva file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dataset is 'SAMPLE_ORDER_LINES'. The required columns to be used in the visualization are 'Profit' and 'Product Sub Category'. The visualization type must be a 'waterfall' chart. The final workbook must be saved exactly as 'Profit_Waterfall.dva' in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\'.
